headline: Freedom of religion tested in 8 cross-faith custody disputes
context: Eight high-profile custody cases involving interfaith families reached Malaysian courts in 2024-2025, testing constitutional protections on religion.

cards.big: 8 interfaith custody cases challenge Malaysia's dual court system in 2024-2025
cards.sub: Children's religious identity becomes contested legal battleground

cards.big: Guardianship of Infants Act 1961 grants equal custody rights to both parents
cards.sub: Conflicts arise when one parent converts and seeks syariah court jurisdiction

cards.big: Article 12(4) requires consent of parent or guardian for child's religious conversion
cards.sub: Federal Court interpretation in 2018 required consent of both parents

cards.big: Children in 6 of 8 cases experienced over 2 years of disrupted family life
cards.sub: Psychological impact on children caught between legal systems is severe

cards.big: Dual courts without coordination sacrifice children's welfare for jurisdictional pride
Malaysian Consultative Council of Buddhism proposed mediation in December 2024
